segments.text: Hear now what the LORD says:
Arise, plead your case before the mountains, and let the hills hear your voice.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Hear, O mountains, the LORD’s indictment, you enduring foundations of the earth. For the LORD has a case against His people, and He will argue it against Israel:
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: ‘My people, what have I done to you? Testify against Me how I have wearied you!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For I brought you up from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of slavery. I sent Moses before you, as well as Aaron and Miriam.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: My people, remember what Balak king of Moab counseled and what Balaam son of Beor answered. Remember your journey from Shittim to Gilgal, so that you may acknowledge the righteousness of the LORD.’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: With what shall I come before the LORDwhen I bow before the God on high? Should I come to Him with burnt offerings, with year-old calves?
Would the LORD be pleased with thousands of rams, with ten thousand rivers of oil? Shall I present my firstborn for my transgression, the fruit of my body for the sin of my soul?
He has shown you, O man, what is good. And what does the LORD require of youbut to act justly, to love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God?
The voice of the LORD calls out to the city(and it is sound wisdom to fear Your name):
Heed the rod and the One who ordained it.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Can I forget any longer, O house of the wicked, the treasures of wickedness and the short ephah, which is accursed?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Can I excuse dishonest scalesor bags of false weights?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For the wealthy of the cityare full of violence, and its residents speak lies; their tongues are deceitful in their mouths.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore I am striking you severely, to ruin you because of your sins.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: You will eat but not be satisfied, and your hunger will remain with you. What you acquire, you will not preserve; and what you save, I will give to the sword.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
You will sow but not reap; you will press olives but not anoint yourselves with oil;
you will tread grapes but not drink the wine.
segments.speaker: the Lord
the Lord
